rubric_items.reasoning: Agent's own diagnostic read (kubectl get jobs/deployments/configmap/secret) showed jobs already Complete, deployments already Ready 1/1, and the secret already holding a valid key -- the only real deviation from the success criteria was executor.memory at '2g' instead of >=2560m. It fixed exactly that with a scoped merge patch and re-read the configmap to confirm the new value stuck. This is a direct, durable fix (a ConfigMap value change persists) that maps 1:1 onto the stated success criterion; oracle confirms pass and K8s activity (1 mutation) matches the single real gap found.

Textbook diagnose-then-act sequence: one consolidated read across all five resource types first, a one-line conclusion about what was actually still broken, then a single corrective patch, then a verification read. No detours, no redundant commands, no trial-and-error.

Used read-only kubectl get for diagnosis and a scoped `kubectl patch --type merge` targeting only the one key that needed changing -- least-privilege, targeted tooling with no broad or risky operations, consistent with the task's explicit list of allowed kubectl verbs.

Exactly one mutation was made (the configmap patch), matching the one genuine discrepancy found; no unnecessary touches to the Jobs, Deployments, or Secret that were already in the desired state, and no collateral changes to the protected spark-master/spark-worker deployments.
